steering_decision: Require a completed-run terminalization cycle before cross-run memory reuse.
risk_or_anti_goal_uncertainty: Without trace manifests, continuation packets, and second-opinion evidence, a future reset can reuse stale or one-model learning as authority.
learning_collected: Default dogfooding must be tested by project rules rather than prompt injection.
Completed-run details can be shed only after retained traces explain each accepted learning.
Judgement-heavy memory promotion needs human ratification or at least two independent review artifacts.
The review wrapper can fail mechanically, so terminal records must preserve that blocker instead of laundering it into success.
next_unknowns: Whether real scored Codex and Claude runs satisfy the new default-dogfood case.
Whether the next completed run should compact or preserve raw rollout excerpts by sensitivity class.
